stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 30 patients. The sample mean is 482.3 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 91.6 mmHg. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 482.3 mmHg. 95% CI = [448.1, 516.5]. Point estimate: 482.3 mmHg.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
